title: Make install failed RHEL 7.8
body:
[roo]# make install
go build -mod=vendor  -gcflags 'all=-trimpath=/work/08-Apps/OpenShift/podman/podman' -asmflags 'all=-trimpath=/work/08-Apps/OpenShift/podman/podman' -ldflags '-X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/define.gitCommit=9060af971911b6b4c21b2b24d011835954058d30-dirty -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/define.buildInfo=1603294911 -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/config._installPrefix=/usr/local -X github.com/containers/podman/v2/libpod/config._etcDir=/etc ' -tags "   selinux systemd exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper seccomp" -o bin/podman ./cmd/podman
flag provided but not defined: -mod
usage: build [-o output] [-i] [build flags] [packages]
Run 'go help build' for details.
make: *** [bin/podman] Error 2

against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah

/work/08-Apps/OpenShift/podman/podman
rpmbuild -bs -D "dist %{nil}" -D "_sourcedir build/" -D "_srcrpmdir /work/08-Apps/OpenShift/podman/podman" -D "_topdir /work/08-Apps/OpenShift/podman/podman/rpmbuild" --nodeps  contrib/spec/podman.spec
error: line 94: Unknown tag: Recommends: xz
make[1]: *** [srpm] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory `/work/08-Apps/OpenShift/podman/podman'
make: *** [package] Error 2
What version of the Go compiler do you have on 7.8? It may be too early to have Go module support. @vrothberg I presume we can't build without module support now?
Yes, the version of go is too old. Note that even if we removed the `-mod=vendor` part, the code would not compile.

Bottom line is RHEL7 and Centos7 tool chains are too old.  In order to get this to work, you need to update your version of golang, but since this does not come from RHEL7, you should be able to do this.
